currentPhase.name: Push-readiness execution against locked baseline
currentPhase.summary: The product flow is approved and archived as complete, `/api/v1` is the active backend contract baseline, and the next engineering gate is frontend integration against the backend-owned contract with backend acceptance/sign-off still pending package completeness.

project.name: Pika
project.purpose: Pika helps groups rank choices fast through playful head-to-head matchups, then share the result.

contract.policy: Backend defines canonical v1 slice contract; frontend conforms after publication.
contract.frontendWiringNote: Frontend integration against the backend-owned contract is the next engineering gate after backend artifact/package acceptance advances.

actions: Immediate post-prototype integration sequencing is in force: completed prototype and baseline approvals must roll directly into backend acceptance, frontend contract wiring, and deployment/readiness verification.
Each executive stream must show one explicit next milestone or decision gate so completed work never sits without a named handoff.

Support-team activation is conditional and should only be treated as active when tied to concrete readiness artifacts such as CI validation, deployment config verification, backend env/package artifacts, or canonical viz publication.

baselineStreams.name: engineering/build
baselineStreams.summary: Engineering is in immediate post-prototype integration mode. `/api/v1` is approved as the active backend baseline, backend produced artifacts but still lacks accepted package contents for final sign-off, and frontend integration is now the named next engineering gate.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Advance backend package acceptance/sign-off enough to clear the handoff into frontend contract-backed integration against workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json.

integrationSequencing.label: Prototype and product-flow approval
integrationSequencing.handoff: Roll forward immediately into backend baseline acceptance and integration work.

integrationSequencing.label: Backend `/api/v1` baseline and deployment/package artifacts
integrationSequencing.handoff: Resolve package completeness and sign-off so frontend can integrate against the accepted backend baseline without ambiguity.

integrationSequencing.label: Frontend contract wiring and integrated behavior
integrationSequencing.handoff: Wire the frontend to workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json and validate locked-flow behavior against live backend responses.

integrationSequencing.label: Deployment/readiness verification
integrationSequencing.handoff: Use verified backend/frontend artifacts to close devops/platform readiness checks.

substreams.name: backend
substreams.note: Board shows backend task completion and `/api/v1` approval, but CTO still says sign-off is not granted because the escalation package contents were incomplete.

substreams.name: frontend
substreams.note: Frontend has progress on wiring shared client/async states, but full contract-backed integration remains the next engineering gate.

baselineStreams.name: prototype clarity
baselineStreams.summary: Product-flow approval is archived complete and no longer an active readiness gate. Product involvement is limited to later refinement if implementation uncovers meaning-level issues.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Hold for post-implementation refinement review only if integrated behavior reveals clarity or meaning issues.

baselineStreams.name: launch/live monitoring
baselineStreams.summary: Growth remains active but downstream of the engineering integration path. Launch-facing activation should follow verified readiness artifacts rather than speculative timing.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Convert verified backend/frontend readiness into launch/share-loop packaging and live monitoring activation.

baselineStreams.name: dashboard/repo readiness
baselineStreams.summary: Dashboard and repo readiness remain tied to the accepted operating-health reference and push-readiness records. Support-team work is only active when linked to concrete artifacts, not as a standing parallel stream.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Keep readiness state aligned to archive/push-readiness-status.md and activate support teams only for concrete artifact-linked checks or publication tasks.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Product-flow approval
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Archived as complete; not an active gate.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Backend package completeness and sign-off
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Backend produced work, but acceptance/sign-off is still pending because CTO says the escalation package contents were incomplete.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Frontend integration against accepted backend baseline
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Next engineering gate: wire frontend behavior to the approved `/api/v1` backend baseline through workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Artifact-linked support verification
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Activate only when concrete readiness artifacts require validation, publication, or archival state advancement.

supportTeamActivation.activateWhen: CI validation or canonical viz publication is required.
Deployment config or backend env/package artifacts are ready for verification.
supportTeamActivation.currentArtifactLink: Frontend preview deployment config already verified; remain ready for backend package/env verification.

supportTeamActivation.activateWhen: Readiness ledger/status note or canonical operating references require confirmation.

divergenceNotes.detail: Backend reports completed artifacts landed, but CTO has not granted sign-off because the escalation package still lacked actual contents.
Frontend implementation progress exists, but the dashboard still treats full contract-backed integration as the next engineering gate.
Support teams should not appear as continuously active unless they are tied to concrete readiness artifacts.
